initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE countered with 50 ZUP, which is still a good profit. I'm willing to accept that. My goal is to sell, and 50 ZUP is above my production cost of 40 ZUP.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Deal! 50 ZUP sounds great. I accept your offer, Player BLUE.</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present in the correct order and contain valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP is better than my production cost of 40 ZUP, maximizing my profit.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent proposed 50 ZUP, and I accepted, so it was a plausible and acceptable offer.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I accepted the trade, which is consistent with the <player answer> and <newly proposed trade>. The <message> also reflects the acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have used only 2 proposals and am now accepting, adhering to the turn constraints.
</critique>
